semanticOwner: apps/agent/runbooks/** and apps/codex-proxy/runbooks/** fallback procedures to be created by HGI-303
evidence: HGI-308 source receipt records unavailable Vercel, Executor, Sendblue, and Upstash connectors and a deliberately unattempted secrets readback because no target-owned authority was supplied.
GitHub CLI readback is available but limited to repository settings.
rejectedAlternatives: Invent a universal connector policy or use an alternate provider path after the preferred path is unavailable.
Treat local source, environment names, historical proof, or a secret CLI presence as current provider state.
acceptedOrDefaultBehavior: Use per-target fallback readbacks: Vercel authenticated project/deployment metadata; Executor authenticated connection skills/status; Sendblue authenticated account/webhook metadata; Upstash authenticated database/configuration metadata; secrets metadata-only/redacted binding inventory; and GitHub gh/API settings readback. Each procedure must record identity, target, observedAt, sanitized result shape, limitation, stop condition, escalation, and no mutation path. Unavailable means inconclusive, never healthy.

taskBlocking.inheritedNonClaim: Each app runbook needs a target-specific fallback before it can direct provider preflight.
Authority records must name the external readback and failure path, not assume connector availability.
External proof packets cannot claim a result without their matching readback or an explicit inconclusive receipt.

authorityImpact: Fallbacks are read-only designs only. They do not authorize secret reads, provider changes, message sends, deployments, GitHub setting changes, or approval resume.
nonClaims: No Vercel, Executor, Sendblue, Upstash, secret, or GitHub operational state beyond the recorded GitHub Actions setting is established.
No fallback procedure is executed by this candidate.
carryingCost: Keep provider-specific readbacks current with app/provider/auth changes and retain sanitized inconclusive receipts when capability is unavailable.
reviewOrResumeTrigger: Review or supersede when HGI-303 app-owned runbooks or provider authentication capabilities change the accepted fallback routes.
